metadata.seed: Well, I have to buy everything in pairs or in twos. And if I only have one thing, I won't buy it. I'll put it back or I'll look around the store for another thing so that I have two things to buy.

patient.query: Well, I have to buy everything in pairs or in twos. And if I only have one thing, I won't buy it. I'll put it back or I'll look around the store for another thing so that I have two things to buy.

automatic_thoughts: I need to buy in pairs

patient.query: I guess I've always felt that if I don't get things in pairs, it just... it feels wrong, you know? Like, I might be missing out on something or it just won’t feel complete. It makes me a little anxious when I think about not following that rule. Shopping can be really stressful for me, and I just want to get it right.

I need to follow that rule

patient.query: Yeah, I suppose it does feel like a rule I have to follow. It’s just that if I don’t, I worry things will go wrong or I’ll end up regretting it. I can’t help but think about all the times I didn't stick to it and felt... I don’t know, off or unsettled? It’s like this weight on my shoulders every time I shop.

thinking about times I didn't stick to it

patient.query: Yeah, I guess when I follow those rules, it feels like I’m doing everything right, and there’s this relief, like I’m safe. But when I don’t—oh man, I just spiral. I start thinking what if I picked the wrong thing or didn't get enough, and then I feel this... anxiety building up. It’s like I can’t just enjoy the moment. I worry about what others might think or if I’ll be judged for making a mistake.

automatic_thoughts: what if I picked the wrong thing
I’ll be judged for making a mistake

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel overwhelming sometimes. I think it’s just this fear of not being enough or making the wrong choice. It’s hard to pinpoint what triggers it exactly; it feels like it can come out of nowhere. I just want to feel okay without all this pressure weighing me down.

patient.query: It’s just that, I guess, there have been moments in my life where I didn’t meet expectations—either my own or what people around me wanted. Sometimes it feels like I’m always trying to prove myself, and if I mess up… it’s like I’m failing, you know? That fear just lingers, and it makes me second-guess everything.

entailment: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, I think a lot of it comes from when I was younger. There were times when I felt like I had to be perfect, and if I wasn’t, it just felt like a disaster. I can’t pinpoint every trigger, but sometimes just being in a crowded place or even just... small decisions can set it off. I find myself overthinking everything—like, I'll just freeze up instead of making a choice. It’s exhausting, really.

automatic_thoughts: not being perfect is a disaster
behaviors: freeze up instead of making a choice

Have you ever noticed that the anxiety comes up more in crowded places or when making small decisions? Let's explore together what triggers these feelings and how we might work on managing them."

patient.query: Yeah, it really can be overwhelming. I think crowded places definitely add to it—there’s just so much going on and it feels like everyone’s watching. And then small decisions… it’s like they become huge in my mind. I can’t help but feel this pressure to get everything right, and it just spirals from there. I think I want to work on feeling better about those moments, but it’s hard to envision how that might look.

neutral: Disorientation as to people, time and place (finding)
Fear of being in a small group (finding)

patient.query: I don’t know, it’s just this nagging feeling, like I’m being judged, you know? I guess it stems from years of feeling like I had to prove something. And with small decisions, it feels like there’s more on the line than there really is. I get so caught up in worrying that I’ll make the wrong choice and then everything will fall apart. It’s like I can’t switch off that part of my brain that keeps analyzing every little detail.

automatic_thoughts: There’s more on the line than there really is
